title: Spanish Seafood Tarta De Santiago
description: A crunchy and flavorful Spanish dish featuring seafood stock and a variety of seafood, cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 400 degrees Fahrenheit
Heat the olive oil in the tandoor clay pot over medium heat
Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute
Add the shrimp, mussels, and clams, and cook until the seafood is lightly browned, about 5 minutes
Add the seafood stock, lemon juice, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine
Cover the tandoor clay pot and cook for 10 minutes, or until the seafood is cooked through and the liquid has been absorbed
Use a mortar and pestle to pound the cooked seafood into a coarse mixture
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and let it cool to room temperature
Once the mixture has cooled, shape it into a round cake, about 1 inch thick
Place the cake in the tandoor clay pot and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cake is crispy and golden brown
